mechanism_narrative: A lower federal funds rate level reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns show no statistical significance against the rate level.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate crossing above 5.0% signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or the signal loses predictive power during periods of stable employment.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized domestic demand growth that boosts corporate revenue forecasts, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform individual series, or forward returns turn negative when both indicators rise concurrently over a 1-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a post-recession recovery phase reflects strengthening manufacturing capacity utilization that improves profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative forward returns during recovery phases, or the correlation between industrial production growth and equity returns drops below zero in post-recession periods.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate profit margins and prompts expectations of tighter monetary policy, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive or neutral when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3% annually, or the inflation signal shows no predictive accuracy in short-term equity movements.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in total nonfarm payrolls exceeding 50,000 signals a sudden labor market contraction that reduces aggregate demand expectations, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines by more than 50000 in a single month
failure_condition: Equity markets rally following payroll declines greater than 50,000, or the threshold fails to separate positive from negative return periods in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence where real personal consumption expenditures grow faster than real GDP indicates rising household leverage and consumption-driven growth that supports corporate earnings, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The consumption-GDP divergence predicts negative equity returns, or the interaction term loses statistical significance when controlling for interest rate changes.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a late-cycle expansion signals capacity constraints and supply bottlenecks that compress profit margins, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive when industrial production falls during late-cycle phases, or the signal fails to predict short-term market direction in mature expansion periods.
